mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of RSAFS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and subsequently S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation and an elevated federal funds rate indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which increases the cost of capital, reduces corporate valuations, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive or flat during periods when both FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL exceeds 5% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts during an economic expansion indicate strong investment and future economic activity, boosting market sentiment and leading to short-term positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with housing starts exceeding 1500k units during periods of confirmed economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and supply chain activity, indicating a strong underlying economy that supports long-term corporate earnings and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant monthly decline in non-farm payrolls signals a weakening labor market and potential recession, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by > 100k in one month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances following a monthly PAYEMS decline exceeding 100,000 persons.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates a broad economic slowdown or recession, severely impacting corporate revenues and profits, leading to sustained negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1% YoY AND RSAFS growth < 0% YoY
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where both GDPC1 growth is below 1% YoY and RSAFS growth is negative YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in the federal funds rate during a period of high inflation suggests a policy error or an attempt to stimulate a rapidly decelerating economy, creating uncertainty and negatively impacting short-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops by > 0.25% in one month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are consistently positive following a FEDFUNDS rate cut of more than 0.25% when CPIAUCSL is above 4% year-over-year.
